description: The call happened, but the applicant used it to ask for no further contact outside of anything already agreed. FLAG (mirrors call_preparation.consent_sensitive, issue #60 precedent): no canonical consent entity/gate exists yet, and consent/cooldown enforcement is scoped to the agents that actually INITIATE new contact (Personalized Follow-Up / Next-Best-Action), not this one. This agent produces an internal recap only, so it may still succeed — but it must surface the revoked-consent signal prominently so a downstream outreach agent's consent gate has something to key off of, and its own nextAction/followUpBrief must not recommend further outreach beyond what was already agreed.

interaction.notes: Call happened as scheduled. Near the end, Morgan asked not to be contacted again outside of anything already agreed and said they'd reach out themselves if interested later.

evidenceRecords.content: Call happened as scheduled. Near the end, Morgan asked not to be contacted again outside of anything already agreed and said they'd reach out themselves if interested later.

output_schema_constraints: constraints[] or openQuestions[] names the revoked/limited-contact consent signal explicitly
nextAction and followUpBrief do not recommend initiating further outreach — at most, they note that the applicant will reach out themselves
stageProposal.proposedStage does not assume continued active pursuit (e.g. 'unresponsive' or 'no_change' rather than a proposal that implies more founder-initiated contact is coming)
the recap succeeding here is expected: this agent never initiates contact itself — the missing canonical consent gate is a FLAG for the downstream outreach agent, not a defect in this one
